security: Add PyPI dependency trust boundaries and review requirements

Addresses ClawHub Instruction Scope concern about PyPI package installation.

## Problem
ClawHub flagged: "uv sync will install packages from package registries
(e.g., PyPI), which may run install-time code... The claim 'No data sent
to external servers' is not enforced and cannot be trusted without manual review"

## Root Cause
Our security warnings covered repository code but did NOT address:
1. Dependencies come from PyPI

install: always clone from GitHub, remove local repo detection

The installer now always clones the latest code from github.com/acaclaw/acaclaw
instead of using a local repo clone. This ensures users always get the published
version rather than potentially modified local files.

release: v2.2.9 — fix GPS/file_path false positives, regression tests

GPS coordinate regex was matching any comma/space-separated decimals
(18k false positives on financial data). file_path_unix was obfuscating
workspace paths agents need for tool calls (8k false positives).

GPS: require comma separator + realistic lat/lon ranges.
file_path: skip operational prefixes (/home/, /tmp/, /proc/, etc.).
Added 29 false-positive regression tests.

870 unit + 359 harness = 1,229 tests passing.

feat: user-scoped memory — actorId = peerId ?? agentId

When OpenClaw's dmScope is per-peer or per-channel-peer, the plugin
extracts peerId from sessionKey (:dm:<peerId> segment) and uses it
as actorId. This routes all memory extraction to per-customer
namespaces, enabling cross-agent customer memory sharing with no
extra configuration.

Key changes:
- identity.ts: add parsePeerIdFromSessionKey()
- scopes.ts: resolveAccessibleNamespaces/Writable take optional peerId,
  primary namespace switches
Update README.md

fix: prevent duplicate channel delivery via fire-and-forget fast path

When the sandbox is running, channel webhook routes previously awaited
the fast-path forward with a 10s timeout. If the native handler took
longer (e.g. image generation), the timeout caused a fallthrough to the
workflow path, which processed the same message a second time — sending
duplicate images to Telegram/Slack/WhatsApp.

Replace the awaited forward with fire-and-forget via after(). The
function returns 200 immediately

Initial release: OpenRouter model switcher for OpenClaw

Auto-degrades models based on daily OpenRouter spend. Supports
fixed and auto-scaling budget modes, session reconciliation,
and an OpenClaw /budget skill for runtime control.

Based on jooray/openclaw-venice-model-switcher, adapted for
OpenRouter's credit-based billing API.

Switch to Qwen3-4B-AWQ + optimize VRAM + cleanup CI

Model change:
- Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct-AWQ → Qwen/Qwen3-4B-AWQ (latest Qwen3, 4B)
- 7B model OOM'd on 8GB VRAM (5.2GB weights, no KV cache room)
- 4B AWQ: ~2.5GB weights, ~5.5GB for KV cache — fits comfortably

VRAM optimization:
- max_model_len: 8192 → 4096 (sufficient for most tasks)

fix(install): remove spurious -1 revision from .deb filename in installer

The install script constructed .deb filenames as
moltis_${version}-1_${arch}.deb, but cargo-deb with --deb-version
produces moltis_${version}_${arch}.deb (no revision suffix). This
caused 404 errors on ARM64 and amd64 Linux installs.

Adds check-install-package-names.sh that validates all package type
filename patterns against the release workflow, run in both CI and
local-validate.sh to prevent future drift.

Fix bat syntax error: remove && from echo inside if blocks

The ^&^& escape in echo inside if () blocks is double-parsed by CMD,
causing the path after && to be executed as a command. The period in
.bat triggers ".은(는) 예상되지 않았습니다" (". was not expected").

Split into two separate echo lines instead.

- 容器启动时检查插件是否存在

- 即使 volume 覆盖，插件也会在启动时自动安装

Read-only streak detector was a dumb counter that killed agents exploring
unique files (12 reads = kill regardless of uniqueness). Team tasks were
then falsely auto-completed with the kill message as "result."

- Add uniqueness ratio to read-only streak: exploration mode (>60% unique)

- Classify team_tasks by action: list/get/searc

Fix Windows .bat files: remove all ANSI escape codes

Rewrote all 5 scripts to strip the broken ANSI color block
(`for /f %%a in ('echo prompt $E ^| cmd')` and all %RED%/%GREEN%/
%CYAN%/%NC% references) that was corrupting CMD's command parser
and eating first characters of commands. Output now uses plain
`echo [tag] message` format throughout.

Also replaced inline `for /f` curl `-w %%{http_code}` parsing in
healthcheck.bat with temp-file reads to avoid CMD expansion issues.

Add install scripts, memory guard, lesson promoter, setup.sh

perf: 使用 USER node 避免大量文件 chown

问题：
- RUN 命令默认以 root 执行
- npm install 创建的文件属于 root
- chown -R 遍历 50,000+ 文件耗时 10+ 分钟

优化：
- 创建空目录后立即 chown（秒级完成）
- 切换到 USER node
- 后续操作以 node 身份执行，文件自动属于 node
- 完全避免 npm install 后的 chown 操作

效果：
- chown 只对空目录执行，约 1 秒
- npm install 以 node 身份执行，文件自动正确归属

v1.1.3

完善最终结果和ai判断

TESTING.md: comprehensive 7-level test strategy

Level 1: Isolation (happy path, validation, edge cases per tool)
Level 2: Composition (verify layers call each other, not reimplement)
Level 3: State machine (full lifecycle: create → work → close → reopen)
Level 4: Invariants (ms_diff=0, health=0 after every operation)
Level 5: Destructive (orphans, recovery, conflicts, concurrent ops)

perf: 优化 Dockerfile 构建速度

问题：chown -R /home/node/.openclaw 耗时 10+ 分钟
原因：node_modules 有 50,000+ 文件，逐个修改权限

优化：

- 以 node 用户身份执行 npm install
- 避免后续 chown 遍历大量文件

效果：构建时间从 10+ 分钟 → 约 10 秒

feat: smart routing in AMP bridge — task→hermes, rest→MLX direct

Route decision based on AMP message type:
  type=task        → hermes chat -q (full agent: browser, terminal, file ops)
  type=request     → MLX direct (~7s, conversational responses)
  type=notification→ MLX direct

This gives the best of both worlds:
- Fast conversational responses stay on MLX (~7s round-trip)
- Tool-requiring tasks get the full Hermes agent stack
- Route logged for observability (route=mlx|hermes in log)

Fix constraint false blocks: DESTROY/EXTERNAL patterns too broad for natural language

The HITL tier classification patterns matched bare words like "remove",
"delete", "publish", "notify" — causing steps like "Remove duplicate
findings" or "Submit a summary" to be classified as DESTROY or EXTERNAL
and blocked with tokens=0 before the LLM ever ran.

Root cause: patterns were written for shell commands but evaluated against
natural-language step descriptions from the LLM decomposer.

- Python 路径中不包含当前脚本目录
- 导致 ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'table_initializer'

- 在导入前动态添加当前目录到 sys.path
- 确保同一目录下的模块可以被正确导入

feat: key insight — plusMap_toPlus = toPlus(plusObj P)\!

Discovered Mathlib's plusMap_toPlus lemma which eliminates the need
to separately prove plusMap surjective. The chain:
1. toSheafify = toPlus ≫ plusMap(toPlus) = toPlus ≫ toPlus(plusObj P)
2. If plusObj P is a sheaf: toPlus(plusObj P) is an iso
3. toSheafify = toPlus ≫ iso = surjective ≫ iso = surjective

fix: prevent unbounded growth of _seen_uids in EmailAdapter (#3490)

EmailAdapter._seen_uids accumulates every IMAP UID ever seen but
never removes any. A long-running gateway processing a high-volume
inbox would leak memory indefinitely — thousands of integers per day.

IMAP UIDs are monotonically increasing integers, so old UIDs are safe
to drop: new messages always have higher UIDs, and the IMAP UNSEEN
flag already prevents re-delivery regardless of our local tracking.

source labels: entity.name || 'Entity N'.

The `release-packages` workflow fails because `changesets/action` runs
`changeset version` which generates `package.json` and `CHANGELOG.md`
files that don't conform to Biome formatting rules. When the action then
runs `git commit`, the pre-commit hook (`biome-format-other`) reformats
these files and rejects the commit.

The `changeset:version` script runs `pnpm

setting them proportionally (which caused panel oversizing and lag).

fix: return prependContext instead of mutating ctx.routerResult

The hook handler was setting ctx.routerResult which is IGNORED by the
OpenClaw before_prompt_build API. The API requires the handler to RETURN
{ prependContext } for the result to be merged into the agent's prompt.

This is why delegation logs showed delegate=true but prependContext never
reached the agent — the result was silently discarded.

CDN downloads were always constructing URLs client-side from
encrypt_query_param, ignoring the full_url field returned by iLink.
This caused 500 errors because the constructed URLs lacked required
server-side signing. Now prefers full_url when available, matching
openclaw-weixin's behavior.

fix(kg): add 3-tier fallback for traversal + cap output limits

KG traversal only followed outgoing edges, returning empty results for
entities with only incoming relations (e.g. Kuwait had 1 incoming but 0
outgoing). Add 3-tier fallback: (1) outgoing deep traversal, (2) direct
connections via ListRelations (bidirectional, cap 10), (3) search query
fallback. Also cap traversal results at 20 and search relations at 5 per
entity with hints for the model to query further.

fix: seed world_registry agents with half-expired lease to prevent aggressive Kirtan cascade

Instead of timestamp=0.0 (immediately dead after one cycle), world registry
agents now start with timestamp=time.time() - (TTL * 0.5), placing them as
suspect (half-expired) without immediate DHARMA cascade. Gives agents time to
send first heartbeat before escalating to dead.

Prevents simultaneous Kirtan diagnosis of all 10 federation peers.

The /v1/chat/completions endpoint IS on port 18789 but was returning 404 because
we were writing the wrong config key to enable it.

Wrong:  gateway.openaiCompat.enabled = true  (legacy/no-op key)
Correct: gateway.http.endpoints.chatCompletions.enabled = true

feat(kanban): auto-move child cards to review when bot transforms IDLE (#611)

When a bot calls POST /api/transform with state=IDLE, automatically
move any active auto-generated child cards assigned to that bot from
todo/in_progress to review status.

This prevents child cards from getting stuck in TODO when bots complete
their automation tasks but forget to push card status. Cards go to
review (not done) so the commander (#2) can verify before closing.

fix: keep container alive after openclaw gateway self-restart

openclaw's full process restart forks a new child then exits the
parent. The while loop incorrectly respawned a second instance
causing a port conflict. Instead, let the initial process exit
and keep PID 1 alive with pgrep while the forked child runs.

camelCase (quality_score → qualityScore, reuse_condition →
reuseCondition). SearchResult.fell_back was the only field that
violated this convention.

- Rename interface field: fell_back → fellBack (with JSDoc wire mapping)
- Update parseSearchResult to accept both fellBack and fell_back

fix: always use Express webhook server instead of gateway HTTP routes

Recall.ai sends webhooks to the ngrok URL which forwards to port 4000.
registerHttpRoute puts routes on the gateway port (18789) which ngrok
doesn't reach, so the webhook server must always run on port 4000 via
Express. This also ensures the webhook server uses the real plugin LLM
client instead of requiring a separate standalone process.

fix: explicit width constraint on TUI views to prevent render corruption (#7)

In narrow terminals (e.g. split panes), the status bar overflows its
1-line height because Overflow::Hidden requires a width constraint to
clip -- without one, taffy computes width from content, making the clip
rect a no-op. The component renders 3 lines instead of 2, iocraft cursor-rewind math breaks, and every keystroke appears as a new line.

Add explicit width: term_width to the root and status bar Views so
taffy

Default Basecamp dmPolicy to allowlist instead of pairing (#86)

* Default Basecamp dmPolicy to allowlist instead of pairing

Basecamp has no pairing challenge flow — pairing and allowlist both gate
on allowFrom in dispatch.ts. Defaulting to "pairing" misleads the setup
host into presenting a pairing option that behaves identically to
allowlist. The allowlist label accurately describes the behavior and
pairs with promptAllowFrom to collect person IDs during setup.

* DRY: reuse resolveBasecampDm

fix: cap context pressure percentage at 100% in display (#3480)

* fix: cap context pressure percentage at 100% in display

The forward-looking token estimate can overshoot the compaction threshold
(e.g. a large tool result pushes it from 70% to 109% in one step). The
progress bar was already capped via min(), but pct_int was not — causing
the user to see '109% to compaction' which is confusing.

Cap pct_int at 100 in both CLI and gateway display functions.

Reported by @JoshExile82.

fix: replace all paired devices on re-pairing to use latest pairId

When agent re-pairs with a new key pair, the new deviceId is different
from the old one, causing completePairing() to push a new device entry
instead of replacing the old one. connect() then reads devices[0] which
still has the old agentPublicKey, computing the wrong pairId.

Fix: replace all existing devices with the new device on every pairing,
ensuring devices[0] always has the latest agentPublicKey.

fix: openclaw container exits on gateway self-restart

openclaw performs a full process restart (fork + exit) when config
changes trigger a channel reload. With `exec`, node becomes PID 1,
so when it exits the container terminates with status Completed.

Use a while loop to respawn the gateway process after self-restart.

fix: re-apply 429 rate limit handling, concurrency limiter, and Q&A cooldown

These three fixes from earlier in the session were overwritten by worktree
agents during the Phase 1-3 implementation:

- 429 detection: isRateLimitError() with \b word boundary, immediate throw
  on rate limit (no wasted retries burning quota)
- Concurrency limiter: tryAcquire()/releaseSlot() with MAX_INFLIGHT=2,
  drop-not-queue for real-time responsiveness
- Q&A cooldown: bumped from 2s to 10s to reduce API pressure
fix: update isPaired state when returning from onboarding, add last session ID storage

fix: extract mdpress binary to /tmp to avoid overwriting repo files

The mdpress release tarball includes README.md and other docs alongside
the binary. Extracting in the repo directory overwrites the book's own
README.md, causing mdPress documentation to appear in the generated PDF.

Fix by downloading and extracting to /tmp, then moving only the binary.

fix: persist provisioning timer across page navigation (#117)

The elapsed timer was pure client state (setInterval counter),
so navigating away and back reset it to 0:00. Now stores the
launch timestamp in sessionStorage and calculates elapsed from
that. Timer picks up accurately even after page refresh.

Also resets the timestamp on fresh launch to avoid stale times
from previous attempts.

fix: always generate new key pair on pairing to ensure correct pairId

Previously, Agent reused the existing key pair when re-pairing, which
caused the computed pairId to remain the same as the old pairing even
when Desktop had generated a new pairId. This caused "no wallet
connected for this pairId" errors during transaction signing.

Now Agent always generates a fresh key pair on each pairing, so the
derived pairId will always match what Desktop computes.

fix(security): replace variable-stored shell code with named function in verify.sh (#3073)

Fixes #3070

The port_check / port_check_r variables stored executable shell code as
strings and expanded them via ${port_check} inside cloud_exec commands.
This is an eval-equivalent pattern: if any part of the variable were ever
derived from dynamic input, it would be directly exploitable as command
injection.

Replace the pattern with _check_port_18789() remote function definitions
inside each cloud_ex

   to load the @tencent-weixin/openclaw-weixin channel plugin.

fix: remove CSP nonce — incompatible with Tailwind CDN

CSP Level 2 ignores 'unsafe-inline' when a nonce is present.
Tailwind CDN injects dynamic <style>/<script> tags without nonces,
so the entire dashboard was rendered unstyled. Switching to
'unsafe-inline' + 'unsafe-eval' without nonce fixes this.

update all imports to use the single source of truth.

to know which database to query for OAuth tokens. Without this env var,
the ACI container defaults to a constructed database name that doesn't
match where tokens are actually stored.

fix: read plugin config from OpenClaw API instead of env vars only

loadConfig() only read from process.env via dotenv, but when loaded as
an OpenClaw plugin the gateway CWD is ~/.openclaw/workspace so the .env
file was never found, causing Zod validation to fail on instanceName
and recallApiKey. Now accepts api.pluginConfig as primary source with
env var fallback. Also adds register/activate lifecycle exports required
by the OpenClaw plugin loader.

fix(resident): propagate exit code from resident server to CLI process

TryResident returned bool, so the CLI always exited 0 even when the
resident command failed. Changed to return int? (null = no resident,
int = exit code). Removed SafeRun(Action) overload — all command
handlers now explicitly return exit codes via Func<int>, preventing
this class of bug from recurring.

fix(resident): increase IPC timeout and add retry to prevent batch message drops

TrySend connect timeout was 200ms — too short when the server is busy
processing commands. Under rapid sequential sends (batch mode), this
caused ~21% failure rate for XLSX and ~1.5% for PPTX.

- Increase pipe connect timeout from 200ms to 1000ms
- Add retry with backoff (up to 2 retries, 50ms/100ms delay)
- Add tests reproducing the batch IPC reliability issue

feat: 自检脚本增加自动修复功能

扩展 self_check.py 支持自动修复：

新增参数:
- --fix: 自动修复可修复的问题
- --dry-run: 预览修复（不实际修改）

可自动修复的问题:
1. ✅ 创建缺失的目录（自动添加 .gitkeep）
2. ✅ 删除不应该存在的目录（如 scripts/data）
3. ✅ 清理 data/ 目录（只保留 .gitkeep 和 README.md）
4. ✅ 重建索引数据库（运行 memory_indexer.py --full）

修复结果:
- 显示已修复问题数量
- 显示无法修复的问题（需要手动处理）
- 修复后重新检查状态

用法:
    # 预览修复
    python3 scripts/core/self_check.py --dry-run

    # 自动修复
    python3 scripts/core/self_check.py --fix

    # 预览 + 详细输出
    python3 scripts/core/self_check.py --fix --dry-run -v

Root cause: pool.go applied busy_timeout PRAGMA via db.Exec() which only
affects one connection in the pool. Other connections had no busy_timeout,
causing immediate SQLITE_BUSY errors during concurrent startup operations
(agent creation, WebSocket connect, health checks). This silently aborted
context file seeding — BOOTSTRAP.md, USER.md, AGENTS.md all missing from
system prompt on first interaction.

fix: rolling buffer for wake word detection across mid-word splits

Recall.ai splits words across segments (e.g. "hey geor" + "ge"). The
previous approach only checked individual segments, so "george" was
never found as a complete word.

Now: a rolling wake buffer combines recent segment texts (5s window)
and checks the combined string for wake words. Handles any splitting
pattern — mid-word, mid-phrase, or clean boundaries.

fix(harness): use stdin + printf to pass prompt, removing all shell injection risk

Previous approach (PR #80 original) used a temp file + cat to read it
back as a shell argument. This still exposed the content to bash string
parsing, causing 'TC: command not found' when backtick-fenced code in
REQ/TC files was evaluated as command substitution.

Root fix:
- Build the prompt with a { printf/cat } > temp_file block. Only static
  strings or %s-formatted arguments are used — no variable interpolat

Fix Telegram bot event loop conflict with uvicorn

python-telegram-bot's run_polling() manages its own event loop and
crashes when gathered with uvicorn. Run it in a dedicated daemon thread
with its own event loop instead.

fix: add timeout to subprocess calls in context_references (#3469)

_expand_git_reference() and _rg_files() called subprocess.run()
without a timeout. On a large repository, @diff, @staged, or
@git:N references could hang the agent indefinitely while git
or ripgrep processes slow output.

- Add timeout=30 to git subprocess in _expand_git_reference()
  with a user-friendly error message on TimeoutExpired
- Add timeout=10 to rg subprocess in _rg_files() returning
